entity.summary: Incorporate a private company limited by shares with ACRA — a S$315 set-up (S$15 name application plus S$300 incorporation) giving shareholders limited liability as a separate legal entity. PayFlip's Philippines-registered patent on its cross-border payment-routing method satisfies the EntrePass innovator track's intellectual-property criterion, so the founders can apply for an EntrePass alongside incorporation.
entity.callout: A registered payment-routing patent clears the EntrePass innovator track's IP criterion.
nextSteps: Reserve the company name and file the S$315 ACRA incorporation.
File the EntrePass innovator-track application citing the registered patent.

visaCompass.summary: PayFlip relocates 14 PMETs (2 founders, 12 staff) — still under the 25-PMET small-firm threshold — so C3 (Diversity) and C4 (Support for local employment) both default to the neutral 10-point score. Because PayFlip is fintech, C1 (Salary) is assessed against the financial-services Employment Pass floor (S$6,200 rising to S$11,800 by age), rather than the general-sector floor, though it's still assumed to be met (score 20) pending the actual offered salary. With C2 assumed at a recognised degree (10), the illustrative total is 50 points, above the 40-point pass threshold.
nextSteps: Confirm the actual offered salary against the financial-services EP floor before filing — C1 here assumes a qualifying benchmark, not a real candidate.
Track headcount growth toward the 25-PMET threshold, since the neutral C3/C4 default won't apply indefinitely.

licenses.summary: Payment-service activities fall under the Payment Services Act — MAS lists Standard Payment Institution and Major Payment Institution licence categories, and PayFlip needs a licensing review to confirm which category its cross-border payment activity falls under before launch.
licenses.callout: Payment Services Act licensing review needed — confirm Standard vs Major Payment Institution status before committing to a launch date.
nextSteps: Determine whether PayFlip's activity requires a Standard or Major Payment Institution licence.
Start the MAS licensing review well ahead of the planned launch.

taxIncentives.summary: Standard Singapore tax facts apply: 17% flat corporate rate, the first-three-years start-up exemption (75% of the first S$100,000, 50% of the next S$100,000), and 9% GST. At a projected S$2,000,000 in Singapore revenue — the largest of PayFlip's demo peers — it's well above the S$1 million GST registration threshold.
nextSteps: Register for GST ahead of the S$1 million threshold already being crossed.

banking.summary: Beyond the standard foreign-owned-company due diligence DBS requires (ACRA documents, signatory identity/address verification, board resolutions), PayFlip's payment-service activities specifically require an MAS licensing assessment before launch — verify status via MAS's Financial Institutions Directory before opening an account.
banking.callout: Payment-service activities need an MAS licensing assessment before a business bank account can move forward.
nextSteps: Prepare ACRA documents and signatory ID/address verification before the first bank meeting.
Verify MAS licensing status via the Financial Institutions Directory before opening an account.

timeline.summary: Roughly a 6-week runway: incorporate in week 1, start the Payment Services Act licensing review in week 2, open the bank account in week 3 (padded for foreign-owned due diligence), submit the EntrePass and staff Employment Pass applications in week 4, and register for GST in week 6 given projected revenue already exceeds the S$1 million threshold.

riskMatrix.summary: Four risks apply: a foreign-owned-company bank account delay (medium/medium); MAS licensing delay or rejection given the Payment Services Act review, the highest-impact risk on this profile (high/high); the COMPASS diversity flag that will apply once headcount crosses 25 PMETs (low/medium); and GST registration exposure, since projected revenue already exceeds the S$1 million threshold (medium/medium).
nextSteps: Start the MAS licensing assessment as early as possible given its high impact on launch timing.
Register for GST now rather than waiting for the retrospective test to trigger it.
